title: Python generated resource primitives for list commands

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by replacing copied helper mechanics in generated list-command modules with reusable codegen-owned Python resource and output primitives.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Generated command modules must remain reproducible from command-generation; do not import package runtime internals; do not reintroduce operation interpretation for these direct projections; keep primitive functions generic and package-agnostic.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact primitive module names, helper signatures, static proof wording, and narrow validation set.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The fix requires moving product-domain behavior into command-generation, broadening to mutation/lifecycle commands, or editing non-generated package runtime logic.
canonical_core.next_action: Add generic generated Python resource and output primitives, refactor direct list command renderers to compose them, regenerate outputs, and update the static proof.
completion_criteria: At least memory.list-skills and planning.list-files direct generated commands compose shared generated primitives for resource-root discovery, resource file listing or JSON loading, and basic JSON/text output.
Static proof checks require primitive composition rather than inline duplicated helper mechanics for the refactored commands.
Generated outputs are reproducible from command-generation.
The slice records any remaining copied mechanics as follow-on residue instead of claiming full #892 completion.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.
